steps.title: Infer the self-interest
steps.content: Keller says climbers spread a false image of edelweiss as growing only in steep, icy, dangerous places. Because the claims were self-interested, the motive should benefit the climbers' own image.

steps.title: Choose the motive
steps.content: Portraying edelweiss as hard to reach would make climbers seem brave and strong for reaching it. A focuses on science, B benefits flower enthusiasts, and D concerns flower distribution rather than climbers' reputations. The answer is C.
